name: Labor Hutments
description:
Labor Hutments — Basic Worker Sleeping Units for Site Accommodation

If your project needs basic, affordable sleeping units for 50 to 250 workers across a 6 to 18 month build, the hutment is the default choice. SAMAN supplies labor hutments in site-built and prefab formats — built around a single decision most procurement teams skip until the quote arrives.

Why the Hutment Is the Default Sleeping Unit on Mid-Duration Projects

60 to 120 workers on an 8 to 14 month highway package — SAMAN hutments close out cleanly at project end without complex asset management.

The hutment is the workhorse of site accommodation — a basic, single-tier sleeping unit for project workers. It is what every colony layout starts with before dining, sanitation, and recreation blocks get added around it.

Four sleeping-unit formats exist in the SAMAN range. The hutment is the basic default — lowest per-unit cost, fastest to specify, fits the widest project type. The cottage is a longer-stay individual format with a more permanent feel. The shed is a bulk format for housing larger worker groups under one roof. The shelter is a quick-deploy temporary unit for remote or early-stage sites. If your project is mid-duration with a tight per-worker accommodation budget, the hutment is almost always the right starting point.

Standard Hutment Sizes and Per-Unit Worker Capacity

G+1 stacked hutments sleep 12 to 16 workers per footprint — the SAMAN configuration when metro project land is constrained.

Three SAMAN hutment configurations cover most project requirements:

  • 10 × 10 ft hutment — sleeps 4 workers · single-tier sleeping arrangement · suitable for sub-contractor crews and smaller worker counts
  • 12 × 20 ft hutment — sleeps 6 to 8 workers · standard dorm format · the most common SAMAN hutment specification
  • 15 × 20 ft hutment with G+1 stacking — sleeps 12 to 16 workers across two tiers · used when project land is constrained and the colony footprint must compress vertically

Per-unit build specification across all three sizes:

  • Frame: mild steel angle and section, welded jig-built · designed for repeated dismantling
  • Wall panel: galvanised iron sheet with internal bracing for the budget tier, or 30 to 40 mm PUF sandwich panel for the mid-tier
  • Roof: PPGI single-skin sheet, or 30 mm PUF roof panel for insulated tier
  • Flooring: cement screed for site-built hutments, cement-board over MS subframe for prefab hutments
  • Door: single MS door with weather hood
  • Window: 2 sliding windows per hutment with grill and shutter
  • Electrical: 2 light points, 1 fan point, 1 plug point — all single-phase

Bunks, mattresses, and personal lockers are fitted on request and quoted as separate line items.

Site-Built or Prefab: How to Pick the Right Build Method

Prefab hutments install in 7 to 14 working days for 10 units — SAMAN delivers from Bangalore and Greater Noida sequenced for site readiness.

The single biggest decision on a hutment order is whether the unit gets built at site or delivered as a prefab. Most suppliers skip this. SAMAN does not — the choice changes your cost, your build timeline, and what happens to the asset at project end.

Choose site-built when:

  • You have skilled on-site labour already mobilised and can absorb the build into your project schedule
  • Site geometry is irregular and a transport-sized unit will not fit cleanly
  • Truck access to site is restricted — narrow approach roads, weight limits, or terrain that rules out trailer delivery
  • The hutment is a single-project asset with no redeployment plan
  • Budget is the controlling constraint and you can trade build time for capital cost

Site-built hutments take roughly 20 to 30 working days for 10 units on a prepared site, depending on labour deployment.

Choose prefab when:

  • Site-readiness is faster than your on-site build crew can deliver
  • The hutment is part of a multi-project portfolio that will redeploy
  • Consistency across units matters — uniform dimensions for layout planning
  • Truck access is clear and ground is firm enough for trailer offloading

Prefab hutments install in roughly 7 to 14 working days for 10 units, including levelling and electrical hook-up. For factory-made hutment units with full off-site production and quality-checked finish, see prefab labor hutments.

Project Profiles That Suit the Hutment Format

80 to 180 workers on 10 to 18 month transmission and sub-station projects — SAMAN's mid-tier deployment zone.

The hutment fits a specific project zone — 50 to 250 workers, 6 to 18 months on site, mid-tier accommodation budget. Three profiles where SAMAN hutments are commonly specified:

Highway sub-contractor camp — 60 to 120 workers across an 8 to 14 month earthworks or paving package. The sub-contractor needs sleeping accommodation that closes out cleanly at project end without complex asset management.

Sub-station and transmission line erection workforce — 80 to 180 workers across 10 to 18 months. Site is often remote, ground is firm, and worker mobility within the camp matters more than fitout grade.

MEP and finishing-trade workforce on a medium factory build — 50 to 150 workers across 6 to 12 months. The general contractor has already built its own management offices; what remains is basic worker sleeping accommodation.

If your project crosses 100 workers AND 18+ months, the economics shift — see the labor colony hub for full-facility planning at scale.

Per-Hutment Pricing, Lead Time, and Quote Inputs

50 to 150 MEP and finishing workers on a 6 to 12 month factory build — SAMAN delivers in 7 to 21 working days from confirmed order.

Indicative starting prices for the standard SAMAN hutment configurations:

  • Site-built 10 × 10 ft hutment — from approximately Rs 75,000 per unit
  • Prefab 12 × 20 ft hutment — from approximately Rs 1,80,000 per unit

Pricing varies with wall panel tier, electrical load, finish, and project location. Standard SAMAN delivery is 7 to 21 working days from confirmed order, dispatched from Bangalore or Greater Noida.

To convert this to a real quote, SAMAN needs five inputs from your team:

  1. Worker count and shift pattern
  2. Site-ready date
  3. Project location and road access
  4. Ground condition at the hutment footprint
  5. Build-method preference — site-built, prefab, or open to either

Send these to +91 97089 89937 on call or WhatsApp, or through the enquiry form. Quote turnaround is within 24 to 48 hours.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the minimum order quantity for SAMAN labor hutments — can we get just two or three units for a sub-contractor crew?

Yes. The minimum order for SAMAN hutments is 1 unit for project locations within South India and Delhi NCR. For project locations outside these primary delivery zones, a minimum of 3 units per batch keeps logistics cost proportionate to the unit value. Sub-contractor crews ordering 2 to 5 hutments are a routine SAMAN supply pattern.

What changes for the worker between a site-built hutment and a prefab labor hutment?

The day-to-day worker experience differs in three measurable ways. Wall finish on a prefab hutment is uniform PUF sandwich panel — temperature behaviour stays within an 8 to 10°C interior differential against ambient. Site-built hutments with GI sheet walls run hotter at midday and cooler at night. Sealing at panel joints is factory-applied on prefab units; site-built units rely on on-site sealing quality. Door and window alignment is more consistent on prefab.

Can a single hutment be redeployed to a second or third project site after the first project closes?

Prefab hutments redeploy cleanly across 2 to 3 project sites under normal handling — the panel-and-frame system dismantles with a 2-person crew in roughly half a day per unit. Site-built hutments are difficult to redeploy without significant rebuild — the cement screed flooring and on-site sealing rarely survive disassembly. For multi-project asset planning, prefab is the build method that holds value.
short_description:
SAMAN Labor Hutments
Basic Worker Sleeping Units · Site-Built or Prefab
Basic, affordable sleeping units for 50–250 workers on 6–18 month projects. Choose site-built for budget and irregular sites, or prefab for faster install and multi-project redeployment.
Size
10×10 to 15×20 ft
Capacity
4 to 16 workers
Frame
MS angle, jig-built
Panel
GI or 30–40mm PUF
Price From
Rs 75,000/unit
Delivery
7 to 21 days
Floor Load
200 kg/m²
Relocations
2–3 (prefab)
50–250 workers · 6–18 months
+91 97089 89937
